page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.1. Purpose
---
The ‘Immunisation standards for vaccinators’ (see section A3.3 (external link) ) are quality levels all vaccinators should achieve to ensure they can competently deliver safe and effective immunisation services.

The ‘Immunisation standards for vaccinators’ and the ‘Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services’ (see section A3.4 (external link) ) apply to all vaccinators, including those delivering National Immunisation Schedule vaccines, vaccines on an authorised programme or privately purchased vaccines including travel vaccines.

The Schedule aims to protect children and adults against 16 serious vaccine-preventable diseases and offers publicly funded immunisation to individuals at risk of COVID-19, hepatitis A, in?uenza, varicella, TB, meningococcal and/or pneumococcal disease.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.1. Purpose
---
Note: The term ‘vaccinator’ used throughout these standards applies to any health professional offering a vaccinator service, including registered nurse vaccinators, authorised vaccinators, pharmacist vaccinators, GPs and midwives. For the purposes of this appendix, the term pharmacist vaccinator refers to both registered pharmacist and registered intern pharmacist who have met the educational and clinical requirements: although the scope of vaccines they can administer is different, the process they undergo to gain vaccinator status is the same. These standards apply to authorised vaccinators, pharmacist vaccinators. For vaccinating health workers, see section A4.3 .

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.2. Health and Disability Commissioner (Code of Health and Disability Services Consumers’ Rights) Regulations 1996
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.2. Health and Disability Commissioner (Code of Health and Disability Services Consumers’ Rights) Regulations 1996
---
It is expected that all organisations and providers offering immunisation services practise in accordance with the Health and Disability Commissioner (Code of Health and Disability Services Consumers’ Rights) Regulations 1996. The Regulations establish the rights of consumers and the obligations and duties of providers to comply with the Code of Rights made pursuant to the Health and Disability Commissioner Act 1994.

The obligation under the Regulations is to take ‘reasonable actions in the circumstances to give effect to the rights and comply with the duties’ in the Code of Rights. The Code of Rights is as follows.

- Right 1: Right to be treated with respect

- Right 2: Right to freedom from discrimination, coercion, harassment and exploitation

- Right 3: Right to dignity and independence

- Right 4: Right to services of an appropriate standard

- Right 5: Right to effective communication

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.2. Health and Disability Commissioner (Code of Health and Disability Services Consumers’ Rights) Regulations 1996
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.2. Health and Disability Commissioner (Code of Health and Disability Services Consumers’ Rights) Regulations 1996
---
- Right 6: Right to be fully informed

- Right 7: Right to make an informed choice and give informed consent

- Right 8: Right to support

- Right 10: Right to complain

For more detailed information on the Code of Health and Disability Services Consumers’ Rights, refer to the Health and Disability Commissioner’s website. (external link)

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators
---
These standards apply to authorised vaccinators and pharmacist vaccinators. For vaccinating health workers, see section A4.3 .

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 1: The vaccinator is competent in all aspects of the immunisation technique and has the appropriate knowledge and skills for the task > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 1: The vaccinator is competent in all aspects of the immunisation technique and has the appropriate knowledge and skills for the task > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
1.1 The vaccinator completes an appropriate training programme approved by Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora. If a vaccinator is working as an authorised vaccinator or pharmacist vaccinator, they will also have undertaken a clinical assessment and vaccinate in accordance with their Scope of Practice (see Appendix 4 ).

1.2 Vaccinators are required to have a summary [ 1 ] of their immunisation practice over the past 12 months.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 1: The vaccinator is competent in all aspects of the immunisation technique and has the appropriate knowledge and skills for the task > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 1: The vaccinator is competent in all aspects of the immunisation technique and has the appropriate knowledge and skills for the task > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
1.3 The vaccinator remains current with developments in immunisation theory, practice, cultural competency, and policy. At least every two years the vaccinator is required to retrain having completed a vaccinator update course that meets the current Vaccinator Update Course Standards (published by IMAC) and have evidence of completion. See authorisation requirements outlined in Appendix 4 .

1.4 The vaccinator maintains linkages with other providers associated with immunisation delivery; for example, immunisation coordinators, outreach immunisation providers, Māori and Pacific health providers, and their local district AIR team.

1.5 Vaccinators are recommended to carry indemnity insurance for their personal/professional protection.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
2.1 The vaccinator is able to assess the knowledge of the individual/parent/guardian regarding vaccine-preventable diseases and the process of immunity, and has the knowledge of the relevant diseases and vaccines to be able to provide evidence-based information to enable individuals/parent/guardian to make an informed choice and give informed consent.

2.2 The vaccinator communicates in a form, language and culturally appropriate way that enables the individual/parent/guardian to understand the information provided. Communication should be supported by evidence-based health information material (see section 2.1.2 ).

2.3 The vaccinator allows time to answer questions and obtains feedback indicating that the individual/parent/guardian understands which vaccine is being recommended and why.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
2.4 The vaccinator informs the individual/parent/guardian about the AIR, including information on the use and disclosure of the information held on the AIR, how the information is stored, and that all vaccinations given will be recorded on the AIR.

2.6 Consent does not need to be given in writing (except for school-based immunisation programmes see 2.7 and BCG vaccination), but the vaccinator must document in the clinical notes a summary of the discussion and note that verbal consent was obtained.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
2.7 Obtaining consent for School Based Immunisation Programmes: For children aged under 16 years who are being immunised at school, vaccinators must obtain informed consent from the parent/guardian. Informed consent can be provided in writing (including via digital or e-consenting) or verbally from parents or guardians. Where verbal consent is obtained, vaccinators must ensure the consent is appropriately documented and recorded on the child’s health file, this may be the consent form. Individuals who are aged 16 years or older may self-consent.

2.8 The vaccinator obtains consent for each immunisation episode and documents that the individual/parent/guardian has been made aware of the bene?ts and risks of the disease and the vaccine in order to make an informed choice about immunisation and the immunisation programme, including the AIR (see section 2.3.5 ).

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 2: The vaccinator obtains informed consent to immunise > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
2.9 If the individual/parent/guardian declines to be immunised/to immunise their child, the vaccinator provides information about keeping themselves and others healthy. The individual/parent/guardian should be advised that they can reconsider their decision at any time, and the declined immunisation will be offered again by their health provider.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 3: The vaccinator provides safe immunisation > Required characteristics of the vaccinator and immunisation setting
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 3: The vaccinator provides safe immunisation > Required characteristics of the vaccinator and immunisation setting
---
3.1 The venue provides privacy and is appropriate for the individual/parent/guardian/whānau. Facilities are available for assessment and management of adverse events, including anaphylaxis (see section 2.3.3 ).

3.2 If the venue is a non-clinical setting (eg, in a home, workplace, marae or school) then a minimum of two immunisation team members must be present for vaccination; one of whom must be an authorised vaccinator or pharmacist vaccinator, the other must be a competent adult who is able to call for emergency support and has a current basic life support certificate.

3.3 The vaccinator can manage AEFIs, including anaphylaxis, ensures that they have onsite access to the minimum set of emergency equipment (see Table 2.12 in chapter 2), and has a contingency plan for seeking emergency assistance.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 3: The vaccinator provides safe immunisation > Required characteristics of the vaccinator and immunisation setting
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 3: The vaccinator provides safe immunisation > Required characteristics of the vaccinator and immunisation setting
---
3.4 Because of the potential for anaphylactic reactions, vaccine recipient (with their parents/guardians if applicable) are required to remain under observation for a minimum of 20 minutes after immunisation.

3.5 The vaccinator ensures continuity of the cold chain and adheres to the National Standards for Vaccine Storage and Transportation for Immunisation Providers 2017 (2nd edition) and the practice/clinic cold chain management policy. The vaccinator ensures the practice/clinic achieves Cold Chain Accreditation [ 2 ] .

3.6 Before vaccinating, the vaccinator undertakes an appropriate clinical assessment (pre-vaccination screen) (see section 2.1.3 ).

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 3: The vaccinator provides safe immunisation > Required characteristics of the vaccinator and immunisation setting
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 3: The vaccinator provides safe immunisation > Required characteristics of the vaccinator and immunisation setting
---
3.7 The vaccinator uses clean techniques in the preparation and administration of all vaccines (see section 2.1 and Appendix 7 ), visually checks the vaccine, checks expiry date, prepares vaccine as appropriate and uses vaccines within the recommended period after preparation.

3.8 The vaccinator provides verbal and written information that is evidence based and follows best practice principles about care after immunisation (see sections 2.1.2 and (external link) 2.3.1 ).

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 4: The vaccinator documents information on the vaccine(s) administered, and maintains patient con?dentiality > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 4: The vaccinator documents information on the vaccine(s) administered, and maintains patient con?dentiality > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
4.1 The vaccinator has had training in the correct use of their PMS, the SBVS, the AIR manual forms to enable them to correctly enter an individual’s information on the AIR (if applicable) and to claim an immunisation benefit (if applicable).

4.2 The vaccinator documents the individual’s personal details, including NHI number, name, date of birth, ethnicity, address, contact telephone number, next of kin details and primary health care provider (if the vaccinator is not the usual primary health care provider).

4.3 Having chosen the appropriate immunisation schedule, the vaccinator documents the following details:

- consent obtained

- date vaccine administered

- vaccine type and number in the series

- batch number and expiry date

- injection site (eg, ‘right deltoid’ not ‘upper arm’)

- needle length

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 4: The vaccinator documents information on the vaccine(s) administered, and maintains patient con?dentiality > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 4: The vaccinator documents information on the vaccine(s) administered, and maintains patient con?dentiality > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
- that the patient was observed for 20 minutes post-vaccination

- if the vaccine was given by a non-standard route (the reasons must be documented)

- the immunisation event in the child’s Well Child Tamariki Ora My Health Book (if applicable)

- the date for the next immunisation in the child’s Well Child Tamariki Ora My Health Book (if applicable)

- advice and resources given.

4.4 The vaccinator ensures the immunisation information is sent to the AIR (ie, electronically or manually) where applicable.

4.5 The vaccinator ensures the immunisation certi?cate (see Appendix 5 ) is accurately completed following the 15-month and 4-year immunisation events.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 4: The vaccinator documents information on the vaccine(s) administered, and maintains patient con?dentiality > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 4: The vaccinator documents information on the vaccine(s) administered, and maintains patient con?dentiality > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
4.6 If the practice/clinic is not the usual primary health care provider, then the individual’s primary health care provider is informed by the vaccinator or by AIR notification within five working days of giving the vaccine, unless the individual declines for this to occur.

4.7 All clinical documentation is appropriately managed and stored to maintain con?dentiality, and is made available to the individual/parent/guardian on request.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 5: The vaccinator administers all vaccine doses for which the vaccine recipient is due at each visit and only follows true contraindications > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 5: The vaccinator administers all vaccine doses for which the vaccine recipient is due at each visit and only follows true contraindications > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
5.1 The vaccinator adheres to the National Immunisation Schedule or approved immunisation programme and delivers all the immunisations recommended for that visit, unless the individual/parent/guardian does not consent to this.

5.2 When catch-up immunisation is required, this is planned with the minimum number of visits/injections and in conjunction with the individual/parent/guardian.

5.3 A dose of vaccine is deferred or avoided only when contraindicated or the individual/parent/guardian has chosen to defer/avoid it. The reason for deferral or avoidance must be documented (see section 2.1.4 and the speci?c disease chapters).

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 6: The vaccinator reports AEFIs promptly, accurately and completely > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 6: The vaccinator reports AEFIs promptly, accurately and completely > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
6.1 All serious or unexpected AEFIs are reported by the vaccinator to the New Zealand pharmacovigilance system, to be reviewed by a medical assessor at CARM (see section 1.6.3 (external link) for the adverse event reporting process) and to the individual’s primary health care provider (if the vaccinator is another person). If the individual/parent/guardian does not consent to being identi?ed, the report should be made without personal identi?cation.

6.2 The vaccinator informs the individual/parent/guardian that if an adverse event occurs, they can also report it to the New Zealand pharmacovigilance system.

6.3 When an AEFI report is received by CARM, and further doses of the vaccine have been contraindicated, the vaccinator advises the local district AIR Administrator so that an appropriate AEFI code is recorded in the individual’s AIR record.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 6: The vaccinator reports AEFIs promptly, accurately and completely > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.3. Immunisation standards for vaccinators > Standard 6: The vaccinator reports AEFIs promptly, accurately and completely > Required characteristics of the vaccinator
---
6.4 The vaccinator seeks specialist (eg, GP, paediatrician, infectious diseases physician or medical of?cer of health) opinion if uncertain about the safety of further doses, and referral is made to secondary care if required.

6.5 The vaccinator ensures the adverse event, and any subsequent decisions relating to the event, are effectively communicated to the individual/parent/guardian and clearly documented in the child’s Well Child Tamariki Ora My Health Book (if applicable) and in the patient records, and appropriate follow-up is carried out.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation that employs vaccinators to offer immunisation services has links to primary health care and to Well Child Tamariki Ora providers > Required characteristics
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation that employs vaccinators to offer immunisation services has links to primary health care and to Well Child Tamariki Ora providers > Required characteristics
---
Immunisation is not delivered in isolation, but as an integrated part of primary health care services, including Well Child Tamariki Ora for children.

If possible, at the time of immunisation, the organisation undertakes other health promotion and/or disease prevention activities as applicable, such as the Well Child National Schedule or Care Plus.

Immunisation events, childhood and adult, are well communicated to other health services linked to the individual (eg, primary health care, outreach immunisation services, pharmacies, occupational health).

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation achieves high immunisation coverage of its population > Required characteristics
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation achieves high immunisation coverage of its population > Required characteristics
---
The organisation has an effective, secure, NHI-based system for recording and reporting immunisations and identifying individuals requiring immunisation.

Respecting the individual’s/parent’s/guardian’s rights to make an informed choice, the organisation takes all steps to ensure that an individual’s immunisation schedule commences on time and that subsequent events are administered on the due date.

The organisation has electronic linkage to the AIR for registration and immunisation event noti?cation, and the AIR is used to assist with follow-up. If electronic linking is not available, manual processes must be used.

The organisation has a robust reminder (pre-call) system which encourages the delivery of on-time immunisation and timely follow-up for overdue immunisation.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation achieves high immunisation coverage of its population > Required characteristics
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation achieves high immunisation coverage of its population > Required characteristics
---
The organisation has an effective communication strategy to target high-needs population groups.

Attendance at the practice/organisation is used as an opportunity to remind individuals/parents/guardians of the importance of immunisation and, if appropriate, to check and offer to bring up to date the individual’s immunisation status.

Those who do not respond to recall and who have not declined to take part are referred to the outreach immunisation service, as per local protocol.

page_title: Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
section_heading: A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation supports vaccinators and AIR administrators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 3: Immunisation standards for vaccinators and guidelines for organisations offering immunisation services
章节：A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The organisation supports vaccinators and AIR administrators
---
The organisation has comprehensive immunisation-related policies based on best practice, informed consent, the vaccination process and management of adverse events.

The organisation uses a pharmaceutical refrigerator to store vaccines, has a vaccine cold chain policy in place and meets the requirements outlined in the National Standard for Vaccine Storage and Transportation for Immunisation Providers 2017 (2nd edition) [ 3 ] for all areas within the organisation storing vaccines.

The organisation provides training and support workers (eg, kaiāwhina, community health workers) for vaccinators working in the community.

The organisation supports the need for vaccinators to have access to ongoing education and training on all aspects of immunisation at least every two years and when there are changes to the Schedule.

The organisation provides initial and ongoing training and support specific to the AIR, PMS, and/or the SBVS (if applicable).

content:

章节：A3.4. Guidelines for organisations storing vaccines and/or offering immunisation services > The service is readily available, with no barriers to access
---
Required characteristics

No fee is charged to the individual/guardian for the immunisations that are on the Schedule or high-risk programmes (or for completing the child’s immunisation certi?cate). Regardless of their immigration and citizenship status, all children aged under 18 years are eligible to receive funded Schedule vaccines, and providers may claim the immunisation benefit for these children. Non-residents who were under age 18 years when they commenced HPV vaccination in New Zealand are currently funded to complete the course, even if they are aged 18 years or older when they complete it. Further information on eligibility can be found on the Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora website.

Immunisations are provided to both enrolled and casual patients, at all times when the organisation or service is open.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：On this page
---
This section provides an overview of vaccinator requirements and authorisation. There are various types of vaccinators in Aotearoa, including those who are approved by Medical Officers of Health and others who are enabled to vaccinate through an extension to their scope of practice.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.1. Medicines Regulations
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.1. Medicines Regulations
---
All vaccines are prescription medicines. The Medicines Regulations 1984 specify requirements that allow vaccinators to be authorised to administer vaccines and administer and/or supply medicines without a prescription for the purpose of an approved immunisation programme. Approved medicines for use in approved immunisation programmes are detailed in Appendix 9 .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.2. Authority for approval of authorised vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.2. Authority for approval of authorised vaccinators
---
A person may be authorised under Regulation 44A(2) of the Medicines Regulations 1984 by the Director-General of Health or a Medical Of?cer of Health to administer a vaccine and administer and/or supply medicines for the purposes of an approved immunisation programme.

Any registered health professional whose scope of practice allows for vaccination, is eligible to complete the training and authorisation pathway and become an authorised vaccinator.

Successful applicants will be authorised to administer either all scheduled vaccines or speci?c vaccines depending on the training and clinical assessment they have completed, as detailed in section A4.5 .

Successful applicants will also be authorised to administer and/or supply medicines approved for use in approved immunisation programmes, as detailed in Appendix 9 .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.2. Authority for approval of authorised vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.2. Authority for approval of authorised vaccinators
---
Regulation 44A(2) stipulates that the person seeking approval must apply in writing to the Director-General or a Medical Of?cer of Health, and provide documentary evidence that they:

- can carry out basic emergency techniques including resuscitation and the treatment of anaphylaxis

- have knowledge of the safe and effective handling of immunisation products and equipment

- can demonstrate clinical interpersonal skills

- have knowledge of the relevant diseases and vaccines to be able to explain the vaccination to the individual, parent or guardian who is to consent to the vaccination on behalf of the individual, to ensure that the individual or parent or guardian of the individual can give informed consent to the vaccination.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.3. Authority for approval of vaccinating health workers
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.3. Authority for approval of vaccinating health workers
---
In May 2022 a pathway to create a new supervised vaccinator role - a vaccinating health worker (VHW), was enabled through an amendment to the Medicines Regulations 1984, 44AA Alternate authorisation of vaccinators. The regulations specify that the authorised person must, at all times while performing the tasks authorised under Regulation 44AA, work under the clinical supervision and direction of a suitably qualified health practitioner.

Regulation 44AA(3) stipulates that the person must apply to the Director-General of Health or a National Medical Of?cer of Health and provide documentary evidence that they:

- have successfully completed training as approved by the Director-General

- can carry out basic emergency techniques, including resuscitation and the treatment of anaphylaxis and

- have knowledge of the safe handling of immunisation products and equipment.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.4. Authority for approval of pharmacist vaccinators and intern pharmacist vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.4. Authority for approval of pharmacist vaccinators and intern pharmacist vaccinators
---
Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators are required to work according to the Medicines Classifications which define specific conditions for each vaccine.

The Medicines Classification Committee has reclassified a number of vaccines to allow administration without a prescription by pharmacists or registered intern pharmacists who:

- successfully complete the Vaccinator Foundation Course (VFC) or any equivalent training course approved by the Ministry of Health.

- successfully complete a clinical assessment

- comply with the immunisation standards of the Ministry of Health.

Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators are not required to apply for authorised vaccinator status to be able to administer vaccines that have been reclassified.

Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators are expected to be aware of which vaccines have been reclassified for their scope in Schedule 1 of the Medicines Regulations 1984 (see Table A4.2 ).

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.4. Authority for approval of pharmacist vaccinators and intern pharmacist vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.4. Authority for approval of pharmacist vaccinators and intern pharmacist vaccinators
---
Pharmacist vaccinators can choose to become authorised vaccinators either by applying to their local Medical Officer of Health or through the national transition to authorised vaccinator pathway (see details in section A4.12 ). Additional restrictions may apply depending on clinical assessment and local commissioning arrangements. Intern pharmacist vaccinators are not eligible to become authorised vaccinators.

Pharmacist vaccinators and intern pharmacist vaccinators must complete an approved vaccinator update course every two years, and maintain current resuscitation qualifications, to be able to continue to administer vaccines.

To demonstrate current vaccinator status, pharmacist vaccinators and intern pharmacist vaccinators must be able to provide evidence of the following:

- completed Vaccinator Foundation Course (VFC), and

- clinical assessment, and

- current resuscitation certification (see A4.12 ) and

content:

- Provisionally authorised vaccinators: This role was established to support the COVID-19 Pandemic response. Authority for provisional vaccinators expired on 1 June 2023.

- COVID-19 Vaccinators Working Under Supervision (CVWUS): This supervised vaccinator role was established under regulation 44AB Authorisation of COVID-19 vaccinator, to support the COVID-19 Pandemic response. Authority for approved CVWUS expired on 1 June 2023 when Regulation 44AB was revoked. The CVWUS role was replaced with the Vaccinating Health Worker role.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.6. Approved immunisation programmes
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.6. Approved immunisation programmes
---
Authorised vaccinators are authorised to administer a vaccine and administer and/or supply medicines for the purposes of an approved immunisation programme.

An approved immunisation programme, as defined in the Medicines Regulations 1984, means a vaccination programme:

- pursuant to the National Immunisation Schedule administered by Pharmac; or

- approved by the Director-General or a Medical Officer of Health

Approved immunisation programmes are implemented as either National Immunisation Programmes or Local Immunisation Programmes. These are approved by the Director-General of Health or a Medical Officer of Health.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.7. Medical Officers of Health
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.1. Regulations and pathways that enable authorisation > A4.1.7. Medical Officers of Health
---
Medical Officers of Health are designated by the Director-General of Health under the Health Act 1956. They are medical practitioners suitably qualified and experienced in public health medicine and are usually based within public health services in each district or region.

For the purpose of clarifying the Medical Officer of Health role in the vaccinator authorisation process:

- Local Medical Officer of Health refers to Medical Officers of Health authorising vaccinators in their district or region.

- National Medical Officer of Health , in the context of the vaccinator authorisation, refers to Medical Officers of Health authorising vaccinators across all districts via the national vaccinator authorisation portal.

章节：A4.2. Vaccinator types
---
There are five main vaccinator types as listed below.

- Authorised vaccinators 1 Includes age-restricted vaccinators and whole-of-life vaccinators. Includes extension pathways for example, BCG and Yellow Fever vaccinators.

- Includes age-restricted vaccinators and whole-of-life vaccinators.

- Includes extension pathways for example, BCG and Yellow Fever vaccinators.

- Pharmacist (and intern pharmacist) vaccinators Includes age-restricted and medicines classifications restrictions. Pharmacist vaccinators can upskill to whole-of-life authorised vaccinators.

- Includes age-restricted and medicines classifications restrictions.

- Pharmacist vaccinators can upskill to whole-of-life authorised vaccinators.

- Vaccinating health workers Supervised role. Includes stage 1 and stage 2 vaccinating health workers.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.2. Vaccinator types
---
- Authorised prescribers with vaccines in their scope of practice. The Medicines Act empowers authorised prescribers (including designated prescribers) to administer prescription medicines to people in their care. Authorised prescribers with vaccination within their scope of practice are responsible for understanding which if any vaccines they are authorised to administer, what populations they can administer to and any educational requirements to administer vaccines. Appropriately qualified authorised prescribers with vaccination in their scope of practice, for example, medical practitioners, nurse practitioners, and registered midwives, do not need further authorisation under Regulation 44A to carry out immunisations within their scope of practice.

- The Medicines Act empowers authorised prescribers (including designated prescribers) to administer prescription medicines to people in their care.

content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.2. Vaccinator types
---
- Regulated health workers administering vaccines under prescription or standing orders issued by prescriber. This includes for example registered nurses administering vaccines in a hospital ward or outpatient setting, or other clinic settings, pursuant to charted orders. This vaccinator group may or may not have completed an approved vaccinator training course. This group is strongly recommended to undertake vaccinator training to ensure that, at a minimum, they have knowledge of the safe and effective handling of immunisation products and equipment.

- This includes for example registered nurses administering vaccines in a hospital ward or outpatient setting, or other clinic settings, pursuant to charted orders.

- This vaccinator group may or may not have completed an approved vaccinator training course. This group is strongly recommended to undertake vaccinator training to ensure that, at a minimum, they have knowledge of the safe and effective handling of immunisation products and equipment.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.3. Vaccinator scopes of authorisation
---
Restrictions may apply depending on scope of authorisation, vaccine reclassification and/or district funding to age groups that a vaccinator can administer vaccines to. It is the vaccinator’s responsibility to understand and adhere to these restrictions.

All authorised vaccinators are responsible for working within their own scope of practice and ensuring their practice environment supports the provision of safe, quality immunisation programmes.

Vaccinating Health Workers cannot complete pre-vaccine screening or obtain informed consent from the consumer; this must be completed by a registered health practitioner. However, VHW can confirm with the consumer that informed consent has been obtained.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.4. Roles and functions supporting the assessment and authorisation process
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.4. Roles and functions supporting the assessment and authorisation process
---
Clinical Assessors Clinical assessors are approved by a Local Medical Officer of Health to undertake authorised vaccinator, intern and pharmacist vaccinator clinical assessments. They are typically experienced authorised vaccinators that have undertaken additional education and are often working as Immunisation Coordinators.

Supervisors of Authorised Vaccinators An authorised vaccinator or pharmacist vaccinator who has at least six months’ relevant experience appropriate to the age range being vaccinated.

Practical Assessors of Vaccinating Health Workers Authorised vaccinators and pharmacist vaccinators with a minimum six months vaccinating experience.

Supervisors of Vaccinating Health Workers Authorised vaccinator or pharmacist vaccinator who has recent and relevant vaccination experience. It is recommended they complete the free Supervisors of Vaccinating Health Workers course (external link) .

- Local Medical Officers of Health refers to Medical Officers of Health authorising vaccinators in their district.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.4. Roles and functions supporting the assessment and authorisation process
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.4. Roles and functions supporting the assessment and authorisation process
---
- National Medical Officers of Health refers to Medical Officers of Health authorising vaccinators via the national vaccinator authorisation portal

Public Health services maintain a register of the authorised vaccinators in their region.

Vaccinator Authorisation Portal – Workforce Requests Portal (external link) The portal is an online platform for new and existing vaccinators to apply for authorisation. It is being phased in with the expectation of managing all vaccinator authorisations over time. The portal is currently used to authorise:

- Vaccinating Health Workers

- pharmacist vaccinators transitioning to become authorised vaccinators

- age-restricted authorised vaccinators applying to vaccinate whole-of-life.

Immunisation Advisory Centre (IMAC) IMAC is commissioned by Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora to provide clinical advice and education to health providers and vaccinators, and training to vaccinators across Aotearoa.

- Practical assessment for Vaccinating Health Worker, Stage 2: VHW2 Practical assessment form - Factsheets - Immunisation Advisory Centre (external link)

- All vaccinators must complete COVID-19 vaccine training course before administering COVID-19 vaccines.

- Complete a Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora approved update course every two years to maintain approved vaccinator status.

This is also highly recommended for scope of practice enabled vaccinators.

a. Vaccinating Health Workers are not required to provide a practicing certificate

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.6. Extending skills/ removing age restriction status
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.6. Extending skills/ removing age restriction status
---
Pharmacist vaccinators and age-restricted authorised vaccinators can undertake additional training and clinical assessment to be authorised as a whole-of-life authorised vaccinator.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.6. Extending skills/ removing age restriction status > Table A4.7: Process to extend vaccinator skills for whole-of-life authorisation
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.6. Extending skills/ removing age restriction status > Table A4.7: Process to extend vaccinator skills for whole-of-life authorisation
---
Apply for authorisation via the Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora Workforce Requests Portal (external link) (see A4.7.1 )

This will enable the vaccinator to:

- Administer national schedule vaccines without the need for a standing order or prescription.

- Administer vaccines to people under 3 years old, under direct supervision, whilst gaining experience and confidence before completing a clinical assessment for a person under 2 years old.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.1. Workforce Requests Portal
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.1. Workforce Requests Portal
---
The Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora Workforce Requests Portal (external link) is an online portal for vaccinators to apply for authorisation. This portal is currently used to authorise:

- vaccinating health workers

- pharmacist vaccinators transitioning to become authorised vaccinators

- age-restricted authorised vaccinators applying to vaccinate whole-of-life

Portal use will expand to include all authorised vaccinators and BCG endorsed vaccinators through a regional roll out beginning in late 2025. Below is a table of dates showing when vaccinators start using the portal in each region.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.8: Start dates for vaccinators to use the Workforce Requests Portal to apply for authorisation and renewals
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.8: Start dates for vaccinators to use the Workforce Requests Portal to apply for authorisation and renewals
---
Until the portal is available in your region, your local authorisation requirements and settings will apply.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.2. Authorised vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.2. Authorised vaccinators
---
- Authorised vaccinators must apply for authorisation from a Medical Officer of Health providing evidence all requirements listed in Table A4.7 have been met.

- Authorisation forms for local authorisation processes are available on the Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora website Vaccinator authorisation process.

- Authorisation is valid once a letter of confirmation has been received.

- Authorisation letters may contain age restrictions or vaccination site restrictions, reflecting the clinical assessment ages.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.9: Authorised vaccinator authorisation process using the Workforce Requests Portal
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.9: Authorised vaccinator authorisation process using the Workforce Requests Portal
---
Apply for authorisation via the Workforce Requests Portal (external link) (external link) (see A4.7.1 )

This will enable the vaccinator to:

- Administer national schedule vaccines to all ages, without the need for a standing order or prescription.

Submit vaccinator skills log and clinical assessment documentation via the Workforce Requests Portal (external link) to be authorised as either:

- Supervised for vaccinating people under three years old, or

- Whole-of-life.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.3. Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.3. Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators
---
Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators are required to advise the local immunisation coordinator of their intention to set up a new pharmacist vaccination service. This is to ensure that local immunisation coordinator is aware of which pharmacies require three-yearly spatial logging of the refrigerator.

Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators are responsible for completing all steps to demonstrate successful completion of the education pathway and are required to provide evidence of this as part of Medicines Control audit processes. Intern pharmacists transition automatically to Pharmacist vaccinators after completing their intern year.

There is no requirement currently to inform the Pharmacy Society of New Zealand (PSNZ) about vaccination status.

Pharmacist vaccinators can apply for authorisation through their local authorisation process or through the national vaccinator authorisation portal. See Table A4.7 .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.3. Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.3. Pharmacist and intern pharmacist vaccinators
---
Pharmacies must obtain a contract from Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora to administer funded National Immunisation Schedule (NIS) vaccines and have them listed on their Integrated Community Pharmacy Services Agreement (ICPSA). Please note that not all NIS vaccines are eligible to be funded administration in a pharmacy setting (e.g. those listed as Xpharm by Pharmac).

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.4. Vaccinating Health Workers
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > A4.7.4. Vaccinating Health Workers
---
Both VHW Stage 1 and 2 are authorised through the Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora Workforce Requests Portal (external link) . Refer to Table A4.10 (external link) for the VHW Stage 1 and Table A4.11 (external link) for the Stage 2 authorisation process. Further detail is provided at Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora – Vaccinating health worker .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.10: VHW Stage 1 authorisation process
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.10: VHW Stage 1 authorisation process
---
VHW Stage 1 - VHW stage 1 has no pre-requisites.

Complete the Vaccinating Health Worker Stage 1 course and Vaccinating Health Worker Stage 1 practical workshop.

Apply for authorisation to work under direct supervision. This means they can, under direct (one-to-one) supervision, vaccinate without the need for a standing order or prescription. Applicants need to provide the following evidence:

- ‘Vaccinating Health Worker Stage 1’ course certificate

- a current CPR certificate in basic life support (anaphylaxis management is covered through Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora-approved IMAC training for all vaccinators).

Once applicants have completed the workbook, and a practical assessment they submit these to the Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora Workforce Requests Portal (external link) (see A4.7.1 ) to be authorised to work under one-to-six supervision.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.11: VHW Stage 2 authorisation process
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.7. Applying for Authorisation > Table A4.11: VHW Stage 2 authorisation process
---
VHW Stage 2 - to enter the VHW 2 pathway, all applicants are required to have:

- VHW Stage 1 authorisation

- completed the Second Checker course

- completed at least 25 vaccination events over the previous 8 weeks

After completion of the Vaccinating Health Worker Stage 2 course and Vaccinating Health Worker Stage 2 practical workshop, applicants apply through the portal for authorisation to work with supervision conditions for new age groups. This means you can, under direct (one-to-one) supervision, vaccinate younger age groups without the need for a standing order or prescription.

Provide the following evidence:

- ‘Vaccinating Health Worker Stage 2’ course certificate

- a current CPR certificate in basic life support (anaphylaxis management is covered through Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora-approved IMAC training for all vaccinators).

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.8. Process when an authorised vaccinator is new to the health district in which they intend to practice
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.8. Process when an authorised vaccinator is new to the health district in which they intend to practice
---
All vaccinators authorised in the Workforce requests portal can practice nationally. Vaccinators authorised in districts are also approved to practice nationally until 1 June 2026. If an authorised vaccinator wishes to practise outside of their home area, they must advise the local Medical Officer of Health and Immunisation Coordinator of the new area and send through a copy of their current authorisation.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.1. Continuing education
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.1. Continuing education
---
Authorised vaccinators and pharmacist vaccinators must complete an approved update course every 2 years.

- The first update must be completed within 2 years of attendance at the foundation course.

- Ongoing updates should be completed a minimum of 2 years since the date of the last update course.

- CPR certification that meets the requirements must be maintained as a requirement of authorisation (see section A4.12 for details).

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.2. Renewal of authorised vaccinator authorisation
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.2. Renewal of authorised vaccinator authorisation
---
Authorised vaccinators need to apply for renewal of their authorisation to their local Medical Officer of Health, submitting all relevant documentation (i.e., immunisation update course certificate and a current cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) certificate). See section A4.12 .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.3. Workforce Requests Portal
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.3. Workforce Requests Portal
---
The Workforce Requests Portal will be available for all renewals of authorised vaccinators and BCG endorsed vaccinators through a regional roll out beginning in late 2025. Below is a table of dates showing when vaccinators start using the portal in each region. Until the portal is available in your region, your local authorisation requirements and settings will apply. See Table A4.8 .

Applications for renewal should be submitted prior to the expiry of current authorisation. Vaccinators must stop vaccinating if their current authorisation expires before a new authorisation letter has been received.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.4. Renewal of vaccinating health worker authorisation
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.4. Renewal of vaccinating health worker authorisation
---
A vaccinating health worker (VHW) is authorised to vaccinate within their scope for two years from the date of the update training certificate. To maintain status as an authorised VHW, authorisation must be renewed two yearly and the vaccinator must meet the requirements specified below:

- Complete a ‘ Vaccinating Health Worker update ’ course approved by Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora during the past two years or within three months of expiry date on authorisation letter

- Provide evidence of a current CPR certificate (see section A4.12 for details).

- Submit this evidence to receive updated authorisation letter.

Applications for renewal should be submitted prior to the expiry of current authorisation. Vaccinators must stop vaccinating if their current authorisation expires before a new authorisation letter has been received.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.5. Maintaining pharmacist vaccinator status
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.9. Maintaining vaccinator authorisation and pharmacist vaccinator status > A4.9.5. Maintaining pharmacist vaccinator status
---
Pharmacist vaccinators are required to maintain their own records to demonstrate continuing vaccinator education and CPR qualifications as listed in section A4.12 .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.10. Process when authorisation or pharmacist vaccinator status has not been maintained
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.10. Process when authorisation or pharmacist vaccinator status has not been maintained
---
Any delay in maintaining vaccinator status means the vaccinator can no longer administer vaccines without standing orders or prescriptions until updated authorisation / pharmacist vaccinator status requirements have been met.

The approving Medical Officer of Health has discretion to reinstate an authorisation at any point, where the criteria under the Medicines Regulations are established.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.10. Process when authorisation or pharmacist vaccinator status has not been maintained > A.4.10.1. Lapsed authorised vaccinator or lapsed pharmacist vaccinator status > Table A4.12: Actions required to have a vaccinator authorisation reinstated after specific lapsed periods for authorised vaccinators or pharmacist vaccinators.
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.10. Process when authorisation or pharmacist vaccinator status has not been maintained > A.4.10.1. Lapsed authorised vaccinator or lapsed pharmacist vaccinator status > Table A4.12: Actions required to have a vaccinator authorisation reinstated after specific lapsed periods for authorised vaccinators or pharmacist vaccinators.
---
- Complete an approved update course.

- Complete an approved update course.

- Complete a full clinical assessment with an approved assessor.

- Complete a vaccinator foundation course.

- Complete a full clinical assessment with an approved assessor.

Vaccinators are required to provide evidence of a current practicing certificate and current CPR that meets requirements details at section A4.12 .

- Authorised vaccinator status is not valid until an updated authorisation letter is received by the vaccinator.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.10. Process when authorisation or pharmacist vaccinator status has not been maintained > A4.10.2. Lapsed vaccinating health worker (VHW) authorisation > Table A4.13: Actions required to have a vaccinator authorisation reinstated after specific lapsed periods for vaccinating health workers.
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.10. Process when authorisation or pharmacist vaccinator status has not been maintained > A4.10.2. Lapsed vaccinating health worker (VHW) authorisation > Table A4.13: Actions required to have a vaccinator authorisation reinstated after specific lapsed periods for vaccinating health workers.
---
- Complete an approved update course.

- Complete an approved VHW Stage 1 or Stage 2 update course.

- Complete a new practical assessment

- Complete the VHW Stage 1 or 2 training course.

- Complete a new practical assessment.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.11. Other vaccinator authorisation types > A4.11.1. BCG Vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.11. Other vaccinator authorisation types > A4.11.1. BCG Vaccinators
---
Only authorised BCG vaccinators can administer the BCG vaccine. All BCG vaccinators are authorised vaccinators with BCG endorsement. For new BCG vaccinators and those seeking regional endorsement see Table A4.14 .

The Workforce Requests Portal (external link) will be available for BCG endorsements and renewals through a regional roll out beginning in late 2025. Refer to Table A4.8 for dates when vaccinators start using the portal in each region.

Until the portal is available in your region, your local authorisation requirements and settings will apply as described in Table A4.15 .

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.11. Other vaccinator authorisation types > Table A4.14: New BCG vaccinators and gazetted BCG vaccinators seeking regional BCG endorsement
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.11. Other vaccinator authorisation types > Table A4.14: New BCG vaccinators and gazetted BCG vaccinators seeking regional BCG endorsement
---
- Complete an approved Vaccinator Foundation Course.

- Be nominated by their employer to become a BCG vaccinator.

- Successfully complete a Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora-approved online BCG vaccination course.

- Complete under clinical supervision a minimum of five BCG vaccinations (using a standing order or prescription).

- Successfully complete a BCG clinical assessment by an approved BCG assessor.

- Apply to the Medical Officer of Health for BCG endorsement approval, providing documented evidence of these requirements.

BCG vaccinators must apply to their local Medical Officer of Health prior to the expiry date and provide documented evidence that they:

- Are a current authorised vaccinator, and

- have completed an online vaccination course that includes an update on BCG.

A BCG vaccinator who is an authorised vaccinator will need to complete a standard vaccinator update course, in addition the BCG update.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.11. Other vaccinator authorisation types > A4.11.2. Yellow Fever Vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.11. Other vaccinator authorisation types > A4.11.2. Yellow Fever Vaccinators
---
Vaccination against yellow fever, exemption from compulsory yellow fever vaccination and provision of approved international certificates of vaccination or prophylaxis, or provision of certificates of medical exemption, are responsibilities devolved by the World Health Organization (WHO) to national health authorities under the International Health Regulations (2005).

Yellow Fever vaccinator authorisation is restricted to registered nurses, nurse practitioners and medical practitioners who must have met strict education requirements and are working at an approved Yellow Fever vaccination centre. Refer to Yellow Fever – Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora and the Yellow Fever Policy for further information.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.12. Resuscitation requirements for all vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.12. Resuscitation requirements for all vaccinators
---
All vaccinators, except vaccinating health workers (VHW), need to be able to resuscitate patients and therefore need to achieve and maintain the following resuscitation skills:

- infant, child and adult CPR, and

- one- and two-person bag valve mask ventilation and mouth-to-mask technique,

- administration of IM adrenaline for treatment of anaphylaxis, and

- use of an automated external defibrillator.

Resuscitation training for vaccinators should cover the specific skills outlined above (excluding adrenaline administration, see below). The use of oxygen, sizing of airways, insertion of intravenous lines and the preparation of emergency medications (except for intramuscular adrenaline) are not skills specifically required of a vaccinator.

All vaccinators must maintain their current resuscitation certification, typically, this is required by completing an in-person course every two years. (Note: employer protocols may require this more frequently.)

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.12. Resuscitation requirements for all vaccinators
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.12. Resuscitation requirements for all vaccinators
---
All vaccinators, except VHWs, need to be able to administer intramuscular adrenaline in the event of an anaphylactic reaction to an immunisation event (see section 2.3.3 ). These skills are covered in the Health New Zealand | Te Whatu Ora approved vaccinator courses and are not a requirement of a CPR certification.

VHWs are required to maintain current basic life support skills including:

- carry out infant, child and adult CPR

- must be aware of the signs of anaphylaxis and have the knowledge to treat it but are not expected to administer adrenaline in practice.

All vaccinators, including vaccinating health workers (VHW), must meet the emergency equipment and management requirements, regardless of the immunisation setting (eg., in general practice, pharmacies and in non-clinical settings, such as homes, schools, rest homes and workplaces), as listed in section 2.3.3 .

All vaccinators are expected to keep up to date with any guidance changes, including infection control and requirements for PPE.

page_title: Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
section_heading: A4.13. Minimum staff and equipment requirements for vaccination services
content:
页面标题：Appendix 4: Authorisation and criteria of vaccinators
章节：A4.13. Minimum staff and equipment requirements for vaccination services
---
All vaccinators providing immunisation services need to have a minimum of two people present, one of whom must be an authorised vaccinator or a pharmacist vaccinator; the other must be a competent adult who is able to call for emergency support and has a current basic life support certificate.

Vaccinating health workers must work under clinical supervision and direction of a suitably qualified health practitioner at all times.

Table A4.16 is a check list for the emergency equipment that is required when vaccinating off-site.

页面标题：Appendix 5: Immunisation certi?cate
章节：A5.1. Introduction
---
On 8 January 2026, the Health (Immunisation) Regulations 1995 were revoked. These regulations required early childhood services and primary schools to maintain onsite immunisation registers by requesting immunisation certificates from parents/guardians of children born from 1 January 1995. These are no longer required as the Aotearoa Immunisation Register (AIR) has replaced this function.

页面标题：Appendix 5: Immunisation certi?cate
章节：A5.2. The Immunisation Certificate
---
The Immunisation Certificate will remain available for vaccinators to complete. When completing and signing the immunisation certificate, vaccinators should ensure that a child is fully vaccinated against the AIR record. Children who have not received the necessary doses of a vaccine or have no evidence of laboratory-proven disease should be recorded as ‘not fully immunised’.

The immunisation certi?cate is included in the Well Child Tamariki Ora My Health Book. This book also contains the record of the child’s vaccinations. Vaccinators should ensure they record all vaccines administered and other relevant health information in this book. This becomes particularly important if the child sees different health professionals. If the child’s book is lost, it should be replaced. Copies of the Well Child Tamariki Ora My Health Book and immunisation certi?cate pads can be obtained from the authorised provider of health education materials, usually the local public health service, or ordered from the HealthEd website (external link) .
